title: An advanced retrieval-augmented generation system for manufacturing quality control

agents.description: Main RAG agent that processes user queries about ceramic tile manufacturing defects, retrieves relevant information from a specialized knowledge base, and generates accurate responses using GPT-3.5-turbo-instruct with domain-specific augmentation.

orchestration.orchestration_pattern_description: The system follows a sequential pipeline: query pre-processing (validation and rewriting), indexing and retrieval (bi-encoder with OpenAI embeddings and Chroma vector store), post-retrieval reranking (cross-encoder ms-marco-MiniLM-L-6-v2), and final response generation (gpt-3.5-turbo-instruct). Document pre-processing is also performed offline to build the knowledge base. The entire pipeline is implemented using LangChain.

human_integration.human_integration_description: Users interact with the system through a user interface that allows either selecting a defect from a predefined list or submitting an open-text query. The system validates queries to ensure they are related to ceramic quality management. Human experts also reviewed and corrected the pre-processed dataset manually. A human expert evaluation was used to assess response correctness (YES/NO) in the generation evaluation.

retrieval.application_domain_description: Manufacturing quality control in ceramic tile production, focusing on defect identification, root cause analysis, and solution generation for non-conformities management.
retrieval.problem_description: Traditional quality management information systems are limited in their ability to leverage external domain-specific knowledge effectively. Existing systems rely on keyword search over internal records, lack reasoning capabilities, and cannot provide well-explained, actionable solutions to manufacturing defects. General-purpose LLMs lack the domain-specific precision needed for specialized manufacturing quality control tasks.
retrieval.proposed_solution: An advanced Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) system built with LangChain that combines OpenAI GPT-3.5-turbo-instruct with a curated knowledge base of ceramic tile manufacturing defect literature. The system includes tailored pre-processing (query rewriting, document structuring), semantic retrieval via bi-encoder with Chroma vector store and OpenAI text-embedding-3-large, post-retrieval reranking via a cross-encoder (ms-marco-MiniLM-L-6-v2), and structured prompt-based response generation.
retrieval.research_methodology_description: The authors designed and implemented an advanced RAG system artifact, evaluated it through retrieval hyperparameter optimization (Jaccard similarity, F1-score, Kendall-Tau distance) and generation quality assessment (ROUGE-L metric with human expert validation), and compared results against GPT-4 without domain adaptation to demonstrate the system's effectiveness.

decision_quality_improvement: RAG system achieved a mean ROUGE-L score of 0.6108 vs. 0.2300 for random baseline, indicating significantly higher response quality.

Domain-adapted RAG system (gpt-3.5-turbo-instruct) outperformed GPT-4 without domain adaptation across all three qualitative query examples, with ROUGE-L scores of 0.59, 0.26, and 0.68 vs. 0.12, 0.089, and 0.12 for GPT-4.
Human expert evaluation confirmed all three RAG system responses as correct (YES) while all three GPT-4 responses were rated incorrect (NO).
Retrieval evaluation achieved Jaccard similarity of 92.68% and F1-score of 85.81% under optimal hyperparameter configuration (k=7, mean threshold).
response_time_impact: The reranking post-retrieval process improves efficiency and responsiveness by reducing the number of context documents passed to the LLM.
The system can analyze all information in the dataset in a few minutes in a fully automatic way during generation evaluation.
reliability_metrics: Mean ROUGE-L real score: 0.6108 (std: 0.1371) vs. random score: 0.2300 (std: 0.05697).
Minimum non-outlier real ROUGE-L score is close to the third quartile of random scores, confirming consistent superiority.
Jaccard similarity of 92.68% and F1-score of 85.81% for retrieval under optimal configuration.

ce_technique_effectiveness.retrieval-augmented-generation: Core technique enabling domain specialization; RAG system significantly outperformed standalone GPT-4 on all evaluated queries with ROUGE-L improvements from ~0.1 to 0.59-0.68.
ce_technique_effectiveness.reranking: Cross-encoder reranking improved selection of relevant samples, complementing bi-encoder retrieval and reducing noise in LLM context window.
ce_technique_effectiveness.in-context-learning: Structured prompt templates with retrieved domain-specific samples enabled accurate, context-aware response generation without model fine-tuning.

failure_modes: Single retrieval may be insufficient for complex problems requiring multi-step reasoning.
Excessive context without post-retrieval filtering can degrade LLM performance.
Open queries unrelated to ceramic quality management may cause inadequate tool performance if not properly validated.
ROUGE-L metric may not fully capture contextual relevance or semantic accuracy in all cases.
limitations: Knowledge base is limited to external specialized sources; internal company records (historical quality logs, non-conformance reports) are not yet integrated.
Single once-retrieval strategy used; more sophisticated iterative or adaptive retrieval methods could improve performance on complex queries.
System currently focused on ceramic tile manufacturing; generalization to other manufacturing domains requires adaptation.
ROUGE-L metric may not fully capture contextual relevance or semantic accuracy in some cases.
The system relies on OpenAI's GPT models, which require external API access and incur usage costs.

identified_research_gaps: Integration of internal company records (historical quality logs and nonconformance reports) into the knowledge base
Adoption of more sophisticated retrieval strategies such as iterative or adaptive methods for complex queries
Extension and adaptation of the system to other manufacturing domains such as automotive, aerospace, or pharmaceuticals
